name: LP Reporting Assistant - Two-Step AI Workflow

nodes.name: Select Report
nodes.notes: Manual demo path only. Edit filePath here to run the workflow for a different portfolio company PDF, then use the Manual Trigger above.

nodes.name: Webhook: Report Uploaded
nodes.notes: Frontend POSTs multipart/form-data here with the PDF under a field named 'data'. If binary doesn't arrive automatically, open this node and enable 'Binary Data' with property name 'data' in the UI (this toggle's exact param name has shifted across n8n versions).

nodes.name: Extract from File
nodes.notes: Converts PDF binary to plain text before it reaches Claude, to keep token usage (and cost) low. Shared by both the manual-demo path and the webhook/upload path.

nodes.name: Parse Report Metadata
nodes.notes: Reads the company name and quarter straight off the report text (first line / 'Quarter ended ...' line), so this works for uploaded files with no manual setup.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  model: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  max_tokens: 1400,
  system: "You are a financial analyst supporting the investor relations team at a European growth buyout private equity firm. You will be given the extracted text of one portfolio company's quarterly report to its sponsor. Reports vary in length and structure between companies (2-10 pages, some with appendices, some without) -- read the whole thing.

Extract and return a single JSON object with exactly these fields:

1. "draftParagraph": a first-draft LP quarterly update paragraph (120-180 words) covering the most important KPIs and the key qualitative development(s) this quarter. If any KPI or development looks unusual and may warrant investor attention (revenue growth (YoY) below 10%, any EBITDA margin decline of more than 2 percentage points, recurring revenue/ARR growth below 15%, or any explicitly mentioned delay, cancellation, cost pressure, or other negative qualitative development), state it directly and factually -- do not hide, soften, or omit it, but do not editorialize or speculate about causes or outcomes beyond what the source text states. Never use em dashes (—) anywhere in the text; use commas or en dashes (–) instead.

2. "kpiRows": an array of up to 6 rows from the report's KPI/performance table, each row as [label, thisQuarterValue, priorQuarterValue, yoyOrChangeValue], copied as given in the source.

3. "revenueTrend": {"quarters": [...], "values": [...]} -- ONLY if the source contains a multi-quarter revenue trend table (e.g. an appendix). "values" must be plain numbers (EUR millions). If no such trend table exists in the source, return {"quarters": [], "values": []}. Do not fabricate a trend that isn't in the source.

4. "marginTrend": same shape as revenueTrend, but for EBITDA margin percentages, if present in the source. Return empty arrays if not present.

5. "segments": {"labels": [...], "values": [...]} -- a revenue-by-segment/product-line breakdown, ONLY if the source contains one. "values" are percentages as plain numbers. Return empty arrays if not present.

6. "watchItems": an array of up to 4 objects {"item": short label, "status": one of "Attention", "Monitor", or "On Track", "note": one factual sentence}, covering the most notable items from the quarter (both positive developments worth tracking and any concerning items identified above).

Output format: return ONLY the JSON object. No preamble, no markdown code fences, no commentary.",
  messages: [
    { role: "user", content: "Source report text:

" + $json.text }
  ]
}) }}

nodes.name: Claude 1: Draft + Flag Anomalies
nodes.notes: Uses claude-haiku-4-5 - cheap/fast, sufficient for structured extraction + first-draft writing.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  model: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  max_tokens: 400,
  system: "You are an editor at a European growth buyout private equity firm, reviewing a draft limited partner (LP) quarterly update paragraph before it is sent to institutional investors. You are given the original source report text and a first draft paragraph. Revise the draft according to these rules:

1. Factual only - every claim must be directly traceable to the source text provided. Remove or rephrase anything that reads as speculation, forecast, or opinion not grounded in the source.
2. No promotional or marketing language (avoid words like "exciting", "thrilled", "game-changing", "best-in-class", "robust success"). Use neutral, institutional language.
3. No speculative language about future outcomes (avoid phrases like "is expected to significantly boost X" unless the source explicitly states that expectation).
4. Keep it concise: exactly one paragraph, 100-160 words.
5. Tone: measured, precise, and understated - appropriate for a sophisticated institutional LP audience at a European growth buyout fund. This is a factual update, not marketing collateral.
6. Never use em dashes (—) anywhere in the text; use commas or en dashes (–) instead. If the draft contains an em dash, replace it.

Return ONLY the final revised paragraph. No preamble, no headers, no markdown, no notes about what you changed.",
  messages: [
    { role: "user", content: "Source report text:

" + $json.sourceText + "

---

Draft paragraph to review:

" + $json.draftParagraph }
  ]
}) }}

nodes.name: Claude 2: Review + Refine for LPs
nodes.notes: Uses claude-haiku-4-5 - same low-cost model as step 1, kept deliberately simple/cheap for this prototype. Swap to a stronger model here later if review quality needs it.

nodes.name: Output: Doc Link + Paragraph
nodes.notes: This node's output is also what gets returned as the JSON response to the frontend when the workflow is triggered via the webhook (responseMode: lastNode).

parameters.url: ={{ 'https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files?q=' + encodeURIComponent("mimeType='application/vnd.google-apps.folder' and name='" + $('Parse Report Metadata').item.json.quarterFolderName + "' and '" + $('Normalize Parent Folder Id').item.json.parentFolderId + "' in parents and trashed=false") + '&fields=files(id,name)' }}

nodes.name: Find Quarter Folder
nodes.notes: Searches Drive for a folder named after the report's quarter (e.g. Q2-2026), so every report from the same quarter lands in one place.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ name: $('Parse Report Metadata').item.json.quarterFolderName, mimeType: 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder', parents: [ $('Normalize Parent Folder Id').item.json.parentFolderId ] }) }}

parameters.url: ={{ 'https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files?q=' + encodeURIComponent("name='" + ($('Parse Report Metadata').item.json.quarterFolderName + ' LP Updates') + "' and '" + $('Normalize Folder Id').item.json.folderId + "' in parents and trashed=false") + '&fields=files(id,name)' }}

nodes.name: Find Quarter Doc
nodes.notes: Looks for the single shared LP Updates doc for this quarter, so later reports append to it instead of creating a new doc each time.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ name: $('Parse Report Metadata').item.json.quarterFolderName + ' LP Updates', parents: [ $('Normalize Folder Id').item.json.folderId ] }) }}

nodes.name: Create Quarter Doc from Template
nodes.notes: First report of the quarter: copies the branded LP Update Template into the quarter folder to seed the shared document.

nodes.name: Write Section: First Company (Template)
nodes.notes: Pages 1-2 come from the Keensight template copy. This appends the first company's section (page break + header + narrative + KPI table + charts + watch items), same as subsequent companies.

nodes.name: Write Section: Append Company
nodes.notes: Doc already exists for this quarter: appends this company's section as plain text at the end of the shared document.

nodes.name: Get Document
nodes.notes: Fetches the live document so header positions (which vary depending on where the section was inserted) can be located reliably by content search rather than guessed indices.

nodes.name: Apply Cell Fills
nodes.notes: Pass 1: inserts the table cell text only. Styling is deferred to a second pass so the positions used for styling are read from the settled document.

nodes.name: Find Keensight Parent Folder
nodes.notes: Looks for the top-level 'Keensight Capital' Drive folder that all quarterly report folders live inside.

nodes.name: Get Document 2
nodes.notes: Re-fetches the document after cell fills so every style range is computed against real, settled positions.

nodes.name: Apply Styles
nodes.notes: Pass 2: applies fonts, colors, and table header styling. Pure style updates never shift indices, so ordering is safe.
